response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior individual contributor with genuine developer-tooling depth (test runners, CI/CD infrastructure, IDE components, MCP code generators) and some mobile experience, but this is a Level II people-management role and the resume shows no people management, no formal technical leadership positions, and no experience leading organizational change. He meets the coding and tooling bar but not the core managerial requirements, making him a mismatch for this opening rather than a weak engineer.
strengths: Deep developer-tools background: authored test frameworks (Trial, Fluent-Asserts), built CI/CD and device-orchestration infrastructure, and worked on a C IDE at National Instruments
15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Teaching and community leadership: created multi-day Selenium/TDD courses, workshops, and conference talks, suggesting mentoring aptitude
concerns: No people-management experience — fails the explicit 2-year minimum for this manager role
No demonstrated technical leadership role (roadmap ownership, team priority setting) in any listed position
Mobile-platform experience is limited to one iOS/Apple TV stint in 2015-2016, thin for a role centered on a major mobile ecosystem

key_factors.factor: Developer tools and testing infrastructure expertise
No formal technical leadership track record
